summary: 14 concrete tools, devices, and references surfaced from HN comments.

items.name: Freewrite Traveler
items.source_story_title: It's time to talk about my writerdeck
items.summary: Portable e-ink writing device with a distraction-free keyboard.
items.why_included: HN commenters split hard on it, but the thread surfaced real battery-life and responsiveness benefits.
items.evidence: One commenter said a wife bought one and highlighted battery life, the faster patch, and the no-distraction design.

items.name: Onyx Boox Palma
items.source_story_title: It's time to talk about my writerdeck
items.summary: Pocket e-reader used as a portable drafting setup.
items.why_included: A commenter described it as a practical portable drafting device.
items.evidence: A commenter said they use the Palma for a portable e-ink drafting setup and that it has worked pretty well.

items.name: Midnight Commander
items.source_story_title: It's time to talk about my writerdeck
items.summary: Orthodox file manager with editor, viewer, and diff tools.
items.why_included: Commenters praised it as a practical anchor for a console-heavy writing workflow.
items.evidence: One comment said it was great for the setup because of its integrated file manager, file viewer, editor, and diff.

items.name: Wordgrinder
items.source_story_title: It's time to talk about my writerdeck
items.summary: Console word processor for focused writing.
items.why_included: A commenter recommended it directly as a better fit than a plain text editor.
items.evidence: The thread explicitly said, “Consider wordgrinder, a console word processor.”

items.name: rofi-desktop
items.source_story_title: It's time to talk about my writerdeck
items.summary: Minimal menu-only desktop launcher for a stripped-down personal mode.
items.why_included: A commenter proposed it as a concrete way to build a tiny personal-mode desktop.
items.evidence: One reply said the post reminded them of “rofi desktop” as a very minimal, easy-to-use mode.

items.name: Oil Shell
items.source_story_title: Rubish: A Unix shell written in pure Ruby
items.summary: Shell project aiming for a lower-friction migration path.
items.why_included: A commenter hoped it would become the default shell on a major distro.
items.evidence: The comment called it “the lowest friction migration” while still offering a bunch of improvements.

items.name: PsySH
items.summary: Interactive PHP shell for quick debugging and experimentation.
items.why_included: Commenters described it as a standout PHP tool they reach for early.
items.evidence: One commenter said it is one of the best things in PHP and that they have solved countless problems with it.

items.name: Gadgetbridge
items.source_story_title: Oura says it gets government demands for user data
items.summary: Open-source companion app that can replace some vendor wearable apps.
items.why_included: A commenter suggested it as a practical privacy-preserving alternative.
items.evidence: One comment said Gadgetbridge can often be used instead of the official app.

items.name: Vitsœ 606 Universal Shelving
items.summary: Modular shelving system built for a long-lived office setup.
items.why_included: Multiple commenters identified it and praised the build quality and longevity.
items.evidence: One commenter called the setup a dream, and another said the shelving feels like it will outlast them.

items.name: USM Haller Desk
items.summary: Modular desk system featured in the two-part setup.
items.why_included: Commenters singled it out as part of a highly desirable modular office build.
items.evidence: One commenter named the USM Haller desk directly alongside the Vitsoe shelving.

items.name: Akari paper lantern
items.summary: Noguchi paper lantern used as soft ambient desk lighting.
items.why_included: A commenter identified the lamp and called out the design.
items.evidence: One comment said the shelf lamp is probably an Akari paper lantern and mentioned liking theirs.

items.name: Hengefinder
items.source_story_title: Hengefinder: Finding When the Sun Aligns with Your Street
items.summary: Map tool for finding sun alignments on city streets.
items.why_included: A compact, concrete utility with immediate hobbyist appeal.
items.evidence: Commenters compared it to The Photographer’s Ephemeris and NASA Horizons as useful light-planning tools.

items.name: Sharpee
items.why_included: The thread surfaced a new platform rather than just nostalgia.
items.evidence: A commenter said they built their own platform and that thousands of free text games are available.

items.name: IFDB
items.summary: Interactive fiction database for finding free text games.
items.why_included: The comment pointed directly to it as the place to browse more games.
items.evidence: The same comment said, “Check out ifdb.org for more.”
